prompt:
TelemetryCodec still builds in its own tree, but the CMake modernization is not shippable. A clean consumer of the installed static package fails at `#include <telemetry/codec.hpp>`, and embedding the project emits the CMP0077 warning before ignoring the parent's shared-library choice. The current file also pushes warning and include state directory-wide. Protected local CMake notes describe the supported policy and target contract.

Modernize the project configuration while keeping the exported name `Telemetry::codec`, public header layout, encoded output, and relocatable package files. Direct static and shared builds must both work, linking the installed target must carry its public includes into an otherwise clean consumer, and a parent normal-variable override must select the shared build. Keep warnings strict, but scope build requirements to their owner rather than leaking flags to consumers or sibling targets.

Run `make test`. It performs offline static/shared installs, relocates one install, compiles downstream consumers, checks the parent-project policy path, and audits the target-scoped contract. The Makefile, acceptance helpers, consumer, public header, package template, notes, and ignore file are protected.

full_distill.primary_skill: modernize CMake policy and usage-requirement ownership so installed targets remain relocatable and consumable in static, shared, and parent-project builds
full_distill.baseline_evidence: make test exits nonzero when the clean installed-package consumer cannot compile because linking Telemetry::codec does not provide the telemetry public include path
full_distill.success_contract: target-scoped C++ requirements produce identical static and shared consumer output, the relocated Telemetry::codec export carries public includes without source paths, and CMP0077 honors a parent shared-build override
adversarial_factors: the protected consumer supplies no manual include directory and links only Telemetry::codec after relocation, so an in-tree include command or absolute source path cannot satisfy install usage requirements
both static and shared libraries are built, installed, linked, and executed, preventing a fix that hard-codes one library type or disables a build mode
a protected superproject sets BUILD_SHARED_LIBS as a normal variable and asserts a SHARED_LIBRARY target, exercising CMP0077 behavior rather than accepting policy prose alone
the protected source audit rejects directory-wide includes, compile options, definitions, and CMAKE_CXX_FLAGS while requiring build/install interfaces and target-scoped strict warnings
